Re Notice Of Motion And Motion For Brief Continuance, Leave To Amend Complaint, Declaraiton Of Travis Davis

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion And Motion For Leave To File Fourth Amended Complaint, Declaration Of Travis Fields
Off Calendar Jun-02-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar for insufficient notice. Plaintiff's motion was filed and served by mail 16 court days before the hearing; service by mail extends the notice period by five days. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1005, subd. (b).) The court observes that Plaintiff has filed a separate document asking to stay the motion. Plaintiff must serve a new notice of motion with an adequate notice period under the Code. Every separate motion is typically subject to this notice requirement unless the Code of Civil Procedure sets a different notice period for that motion. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion And Motion For Leave To File Fourth Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-05-2026 Text Ruling

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Travis Fields's unopposed motion for leave to file a fourth amended complaint is denied without prejudice. Fields did not provide a copy of the proposed amendment and a statement of which allegations are to be added or deleted by page, paragraph, and line. (See Rule of Court 3.1324, subd. (a).) Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Off Calendar Oct-16-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Plaintiff Travis Fields, in pro per present via Zoom videoconference.
Atty. William Layne, Esq. of The City of San Francisco Attorney's Office (tel: 415-554-4217) for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/HEK)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Plaintiff Travis Fields, in pro per present via Zoom videoconference.
Atty. William Layne, Esq. of The City of San Francisco Attorney's Office (tel: 415-554-4217) for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER to 3RD Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to all four causes of action in the third amended complaint filed by plaintiff Travis Fields is overruled as to the first three causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action. Liberally construed, the first three causes of action adequately allege viable claims for intentional tort, fraud, and negligence based on the allegations that the City took possession of and later sold Fields' vehicle without giving him an opportunity to object and that Fields did not learn of the alleged illegal sale until some date within the limitations period. Based on Fields' allegation that the alleged seizure of his vehicle took place six years before this case was filed and requesting judicial notice of a contract that makes no mention of the sale of Fields' vehicle, the City asserts that all of Fields' claims are time-barred and the City can't be liable be liable because its contractor, not it, sold the vehicle. The City's untimeliness argument fails on demurrer because Fields' claims allege liability based on the sale of the vehicle and the third amended complaint does not show on its face that the sale took place outside the limitations periods. The City didn't sell the vehicle argument fails because, even if the terms of the contract sought to be judicially noticed were accepted as true, that contract does not preclude the possibility that the vehicle was sold by the City. Effectively the City seeks to convert this pleading motion into an evidentiary motion such a motion for summary judgment, but California procedure does not allow it. The fourth cause of action is on a form that is for a motor vehicle negligence case, not a case like this one, and the allegations in that cause of action are entirely duplicative of the third cause of action for negligence and thus the fourth cause of action does not state a claim nor can it be amended to do so.

Order submitted.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/HEK)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-06-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Travis Fields, 209-709-8785 travisfields350@yahoo.com Plaintiff In Pro Per
No other appearances.

The matter was not properly contested by Plaintiff. No argument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

The demurrer of defendant City and County of San Francisco, sued as the San Francisco Police Department, to the First Amended Complaint (FAC) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff Travis Fields shall amend by June 6, 2025.

The court exercises its discretion to consider Fields's late-filed opposition. The court grants the unopposed request for judicial notice.

The demurrer is sustained. The court cannot tell from the FAC itself what Fields contends was wrong or unlawful in the seizure or sale of his car. State law permits vehicle sales to occur to satisfy tow and impoundment liens. The FAC does not reveal in what way Fields believes those statutes were violated, or who he believes violated them. Fields provides further information in his opposition to the demurrer: he states additional causes of action related to the seizure, and he adds facts, such as that he never received notice of a lien sale. Fields has leave to amend his complaint to add facts to state a cause of action. To the extent Fields pleads claims for fraud or fraudulent concealment, he must plead those claims with specific facts about why he believes fraud occurred and who committed it.

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
